summary:
This bill designates Holcombe Rucker Park in Harlem, New York, as the “Holcombe Rucker Park National Commemorative Site.” The designation is intended to recognize the park’s historic role in the development of basketball and the achievements of Holcombe Rucker, as well as the many talented players associated with the park.

The Secretary of the Interior is authorized to enter into cooperative agreements with public or private entities, including the Mayor of New York City or a designee, for interpretive and educational purposes. These purposes may include installing a plaque, interpretive exhibit, or other marker at the site, provided the site’s owner gives written consent.

The bill explicitly states that the commemorative site is not a unit of the National Park System. It also imposes several limitations: the Secretary of the Interior may not interfere with private property rights, local zoning ordinances, or land use plans of New York State or its political subdivisions; the designation does not affect the administration of the park by New York City or New York State; and the Secretary is not authorized to acquire any land or interests in land through condemnation or any other means.

The Act was approved on January 4, 2025.
